title: On the Dignity of Obscurity
author: From: Mark Twain
content: Publicity is a form of vanity. The most meaningful work is often done in total obscurity, where the artist is accountable only to the work itself.
action: Action: Complete one task today without telling anyone about it or seeking feedback.

title: The OODA Loop (Refined)
author: From: John Boyd
content: Observe, Orient, Decide, Act. The 'Orient' phase is the most critical—it is where you shape your perspective to filter reality correctly.
action: Action: Spend 5 minutes before starting work explicitly 'orienting'—remind yourself of the goal and the potential pitfalls.

title: The Pareto Principle (New Application)
author: From: Richard Koch
content: The 80/20 rule doesn't just apply to output; it applies to your stress. 80% of your current frustration likely stems from 20% of your tasks.
action: Action: Identify the one task causing you the most stress and delegate or delete it.

title: The Law of Least Effort
author: From: Cognitive Science
content: People follow the path of least resistance. If you want to change a habit, make the 'good' choice the one that requires the least friction.
action: Action: Prepare your workspace tonight for tomorrow’s task so the start is frictionless.

title: The Endowment Effect
author: From: Dan Ariely
content: We fall in love with our own ideas simply because they are ours. This prevents us from seeing better alternatives.
action: Action: Take an idea you’ve been championing and argue *against* it for 5 minutes.
